saved sidecar: models/qwen3.6-27b/qwen3.6-27b.mq4.triattn.blended_v3.bin
Mean Resultant Length R_f across all (layer, head, band): mean=0.464, median=0.427, 3.7% > 0.95
paper target: ~90% of heads R > 0.95 (Figure 2C)
note: R_f is validation-prompt-INDEPENDENT (a plus) but has LOW DYNAMIC RANGE — empirically on qwen3.5-9b, calibration corpora of 20k / 50k / 100k Wikipedia tokens all land at R_f≈0.74 despite the 5× size difference. Treat R_f as a floor check (is aggregation converging at all?), not as a ranking metric.

=== overall mean r̄ across FA layers: 0.610 ===
paper target: ≈0.5 (Figure 3 mean), per-head 0.6-0.9 common; calibration corpus is tiny here
note: r̄ plateaus by ~20k calibration tokens under running-mean aggregation — more data does NOT improve r̄ past that point. r̄ is also validation-prompt-dependent; for sidecar quality decisions, validate against a downstream long-context recall test rather than this number alone.
